metric_summaries_ui.spend_to_income_ratio: While currently healthy, your ratio has been volatile, sometimes exceeding 70%. Reviewing your shopping and food spend could help maintain stability.
metric_summaries_ui.saving_consistency: Moderate savings consistency means missed opportunities. Reviewing your shopping spend, which has been rising, could significantly improve regularity.
metric_summaries_ui.emergency_corpus: Your emergency cushion is significantly below target, leaving you vulnerable to unexpected expenses. Building this buffer is crucial.
metric_summaries_ui.emi_burden: Keeping your loan repayments manageable ensures financial flexibility, allowing you to allocate more funds towards savings or other important goals.
metric_summaries_ui.credit_score: A stronger credit score can unlock better interest rates and loan options, significantly reducing your borrowing costs over time.
metric_summaries_ui.life_insurance: Your life cover falls well short of the recommended level, but your positive monthly surplus provides a path to gradually close this protection gap.
metric_summaries_ui.health_insurance: Your health cover falls short of the recommended amount, but your positive monthly surplus offers a clear path to increase your protection.
metric_summaries_ui.tax_filing_status: Filing your taxes ensures compliance with regulations and is essential for claiming refunds or utilizing various tax benefits.
metric_summaries_ui.tax_savings: A low tax optimization score means you might be missing out on significant savings through available deductions and exemptions.
metric_summaries_ui.investment_rate: An early-stage investment rate means your wealth growth might be slower than desired, impacting long-term financial goals.
metric_summaries_ui.portfolio_diversification: A diversified portfolio helps spread risk across different asset classes, potentially offering more stable returns over time.
metric_summaries_ui.portfolio_overlap: Without overlap data, it's hard to tell if your funds are duplicating investments, which could reduce true diversification benefits.

spend_to_income_ratio.text: Track your spending to identify areas for better control.

saving_consistency.text: Set up auto-debit for savings to build a consistent habit.

emergency_corpus.text: Start building your emergency fund to reach the recommended target.

emi_burden.text: Consider prepaying loans to further reduce your repayment commitments.

credit_score.text: Check your credit report to understand how to improve your score.

life_insurance.text: Increase your life cover to better protect your family's future.

health_insurance.text: Review and increase your health cover for better medical security.

tax_filing_status.text: Ensure timely filing of your Income Tax Returns annually.

tax_savings.text: Explore options like PPF, NPS, and Life Insurance for tax savings.

investment_rate.text: Increase your monthly investment to accelerate wealth creation.

portfolio_diversification.text: Review your asset allocation to align with your financial goals.

portfolio_overlap.text: Provide fund details to check for potential investment overlaps.

overall_summary.overview: Your loan repayments are healthy at 17% of income, providing a stable financial base. However, protection, tax efficiency, and savings discipline require focused attention for your household.
whats_going_well: Your loan repayment load is well-managed at 17% of income, ensuring financial flexibility.
Your credit profile is good at 712 and shows consistent improvement, enhancing future borrowing options.
Your latest spending-to-income ratio is healthy at 69%, indicating effective expense control.
Your investment portfolio is well-diversified across asset classes, spreading risk effectively.
whats_needs_attention: Your life and health covers are significantly below recommended levels, leaving your family vulnerable.
Your tax optimization is very low (1.2 out of 5), missing opportunities to reduce taxable income.
While improving, your credit profile at 712 still has room to reach the excellent tier for better terms.
Your emergency cushion is critically low at 1.2 months, inadequate for unexpected financial shocks.
Savings consistency is moderate, with only 6 out of 12 months meeting the threshold, impacting wealth building.
Your investment commitment is early-stage at 6% of income, slowing your long-term wealth accumulation.
